translated: I would like to take a look at the card illustrations now.
First, I have some comments from the illustration team regarding the outfits to share with you.
This story was one where Honami reaffirmed the importance and weight of making decisions as a leader.

While it focuses on her struggles as a leader, the outfit is designed to reflect her positive attitude in finding the best path for Leoni. Inspired by the rhythmic, light-footed feel of the song, we went with a pop and cute color palette that is rare for Leoni, using casual sneakers to create a sweet and charming style.
Also, to represent the idea of choosing a path while moving forward, all three characters have a star chart or a similar motif,
specifically an item where the color palette is designed to look like a compass.
The color palette was inspired by the choice between Leoni's new song and Miku's song.
It represents the two choices they faced this time, as well as the many choices that will continue into the future. With that said, let's take a look.
Now, where should I start?
Which one should we look at first?
Do we usually start with the 4-stars?
Let's go with the 4-stars then.
Here is Honami.
This is the card illustration that appeared in Episode 8 earlier,
and this is the untrained version.
Honami has this surprised look on her face, but that smartphone case is so cute and soothing.
Is that a Shiba Inu on it?
It really is very relaxing to look at.
And here is the trained version.
Yeah, this is nice.
It has a vertical composition, and it looks even better when you see it on a vertical screen.
It's true that Leoni hasn't really had anything this pop and cute before.
Right?
There are so many stars, it's adorable.
Oh, look, there's a dog on the sign of the building in the back.
Nice, and she has the compass attached to her bag too.
So stylish.
So, that was Honami's trained illustration.
And her pin is even an apple!
How cute.
It's great, isn't it?
Okay, next is Ichika.
Here is Ichika's untrained version.
This was from Episode 7.
It's the scene where Ichika tells her she doesn't need to worry so much.
Yes.
And next, here is the trained version.
Wow.
I lose all my vocabulary when something is this good.
I can only say "Wow."
I love how you can see the whole scene in Ichika's version too.
And the way she's sitting with her knees up while wearing her guitar is just perfect.
We call that "triangle sitting," right?
Wait, is that what it's called?
Oh, my regional dialect just slipped out.
Is "triangle sitting" only a Kansai thing?
That's what I call it.
Have you never heard of it?
Oh, you haven't?
No?
Really?
Triangle sitting.
I guess not.
Sorry, I meant sitting with your knees hugged.
Wait.
Huh.
Is it a dialect?
My apologies.
But doesn't sitting like that feel very student-like?
You don't really sit with your knees hugged like that once you're an adult.
I only do it during photo shoots.
When they tell me to sit down compactly, I'll sit like that, but yeah.
People say "gym sitting," but apparently "triangle sitting" is limited to Kansai.
I'm so sorry.
Excuse me.
Anyway, I personally really like it because it feels so much like a student.
Also, there are a lot of instruments on the signs.
Well, like...
Wait, aren't there a ton of them?
There's a guitar, a bass, and I can see an acoustic guitar too.
You can even see a bit of the drums.
This is just so cute.
Yes.
So, that was Ichika.
Next is Rin-chan.
Here is the cute untrained Rin-chan.
She's all excited for the party.
It's so good.
Leoni's Rin-chan is just great, isn't she?
I really love how she's just oozing those gal vibes.
